macron.name: Emmanuel Macron
macron.bio: Former Rothschild banker who won the presidency at 39, Macron made possibly the worst political gamble in modern French history when he dissolved the National Assembly in June 2024 after RN crushed his party in European elections—resulting in a hung parliament and unprecedented chaos with FOUR prime ministers (Barnier, Bayrou, Lecornu I & II) in under a year, two toppled by censure motions. He admitted in his New Year's address that the dissolution 'brought more division than solutions,' a rare concession from Jupiter himself. His second term has been defined by the forced-through pension reform (massive protests in 2023), losing his parliamentary majority, and becoming increasingly isolated as a lame duck president who can't run again in 2027. By early 2026, he's clawed back some international relevance by standing up to Trump over Greenland and Denmark, earning brief 'cool Macron in sunglasses' media moments, but domestically every ambitious politician from Philippe to Bardella is already running for 2027 to replace him while he presides over France's worst political crisis since the Fifth Republic began.
macron.meme_summary: The perfect target for 'out-of-touch elite' mockery with his condescending tone, Jupiterian self-image, and endless supply of cringe-worthy quotes.
macron.best_takedown_angle: Mock his corporate background and banker attitude—portray him as a rich guy who thinks he knows better than ordinary people. Emphasize his dismissive tone toward critics.
visual_gags: Walking away from protests like a boss
Looking down his nose at plebs
catchphrases: Les Gaulois sont réfractaires au changement
La meilleure façon de se payer un costard c'est de travailler
macron.combo_partners: Mélenchon (two delusional egos clashing), Le Pen (elitist vs populist showdown), Valls (the two comeback kids nobody wanted)
macron.target_audience: Right-wing anti-globalists, yellow vests, working class, nationalist youth

lecornu.name: Sébastien Lecornu
lecornu.bio: The ultimate political survivor: Lecornu has been in every single government since 2017, serving as Overseas Minister, Defense Minister, and finally Prime Minister—appointed on September 9, 2025 after Bayrou was toppled by a censure vote. In a surreal twist, he resigned after just 26 days when his first cabinet was rejected, only for Macron to immediately reappoint him on October 10 to form a SECOND government—making him the PM who got fired and rehired in the same week. His signature achievement was finally ramming through the 2026 budget in February, surviving two no-confidence votes thanks to PS abstaining in exchange for concessions like €1 university lunches. He also announced the end of 'lifetime benefits' for former ministers as of January 2026, a populist move nobody expected from a career technocrat. Now France's fourth PM in barely a year, he embodies Macron's desperate strategy of recycling loyal technocrats while the political system burns—but at least he got a budget passed.
lecornu.meme_summary: The ultimate political survivor who somehow keeps getting promoted despite being completely forgettable—like a video game NPC who accidentally became final boss.
lecornu.best_takedown_angle: Highlight his blandness and lack of charisma—he's a manager promoted beyond his competence. Mock the 'Fnac guy' trying to run a country.
lecornu.combo_partners: Macron (the boring PM serving the arrogant President), Retailleau (two security hardliners with no personality)
lecornu.target_audience: Anti-establishment voters, meme-literate youth, political cynics

borne.name: Elisabeth Borne
borne.role: Ministre de l'Éducation nationale, de l'Enseignement supérieur et de la Recherche
borne.bio: The former PM (2022-2024) who will forever be remembered as the woman who rammed through Macron's deeply unpopular pension reform using Article 49.3 ELEVEN times, triggering France's largest street protests in decades. After being replaced by Gabriel Attal in January 2024, she made an unexpected comeback as Education Minister in Bayrou's government (December 2024-October 2025), handling the Bétharram school abuse scandal where she was forced to admit 'the State was not there.' Left the government when Lecornu's second cabinet reshuffled, returning once again to the backbenches. An engineer by training and the second woman to serve as French PM, she's the technocrat who took all the political bullets for Macron's reform agenda, got briefly recycled as Education Minister, and was quietly discarded again.
borne.meme_summary: The 49.3 queen who bypassed democracy so many times it became a meme—she's the face of 'we know better than voters' governance.
borne.best_takedown_angle: Mock her democratic shortcuts and bureaucratic arrogance. She represents everything wrong with technocratic governance—imposing unpopular policies without debate.
visual_gags: Holding up papers with '49.3' written on them
Serious expression while protesters rage outside
borne.combo_partners: Macron (the duo that forced through pension reform), Lecornu (two recycled ministers who won't go away)
borne.target_audience: Pension reform protesters, students, anti-Macron left and right

attal.name: Gabriel Attal
attal.role: Chef de file de Renaissance à l'Assemblée / Ancien Premier Ministre
attal.bio: The youngest Prime Minister in French history (34 when appointed in January 2024) and France's first openly gay head of government—who lasted just eight months before being replaced in September 2024. He went from Education Minister (where he banned the abaya in schools) to PM, then became the youngest ex-PM since Napoleon. Now heading the Renaissance party as General Secretary since December 2024, he's openly preparing a presidential bid for 2027 with an AI-driven campaign strategy and plans to rename the party after the 2026 municipal elections. In January 2026, he hosted a 'Nouvelle République' event signaling his presidential ambitions while frustrating PM Lecornu by not fully backing the government. Describes himself as 'liberal on economics, firm on security, progressive on social issues' - basically trying to be everything to everyone while Macron's legacy burns around him.
attal.meme_summary: The boy wonder who went from banning abayas to running France in record time, then got fired in record time—the shortest PM tenure since 19th century.
attal.best_takedown_angle: Mock his meteoric rise and fall—he was PM for less than a year. The youngest leader in French history who couldn't even finish a full term before being discarded by his own president.
visual_gags: Walking out of Matignon with boxes
Looking too young for the job
attal.combo_partners: Macron (the young protégé and his president), Philippe (two ex-PMs circling 2027), Hidalgo (the Paris vs national contrast)
attal.target_audience: Young voters, Macron loyalists, political observers, anyone who enjoys watching rising stars crash

darmanin.name: Gérald Darmanin
darmanin.role: Garde des Sceaux, Ministre de la Justice
darmanin.bio: The political chameleon from Tourcoing who served as Interior Minister from 2020-2024 (passing the controversial 'Loi Darmanin' on immigration) before being reshuffled to Justice Minister (Garde des Sceaux) in the Lecornu government. In January 2026, he launched his pre-2027 bid with a bombshell proposal: a 2-3 year total moratorium on legal immigration including family reunification and work permits, followed by a quota system determined by public referendum. The proposal was widely seen as a presidential campaign trial balloon rather than actual policy. The guy who went from Socialist Party member in his youth to implementing France's most restrictive immigration policies—his political trajectory is basically a GPS that recalculates toward wherever power is heading. Critics point out he oversaw 1.4 million immigrants entering France during his Interior Ministry tenure, making his hardline pivot a masterclass in hypocrisy.
darmanin.meme_summary: The minister who never leaves—like a cockroach surviving political apocalypse, always finding a new portfolio when the cabinet reshuffles.
darmanin.best_takedown_angle: Mock his opportunism and lack of principles—he switches positions faster than a weathervane. The Tourcoing boy who forgot where he came from.
catchphrases: Je ne suis pas un ministre de droite
darmanin.combo_partners: Retailleau (dueling hardliners), Macron (the master and his chameleon), Zemmour (two guys obsessed with security)
darmanin.target_audience: Right-wing security voters, anti-immigration crowd, law-and-order advocates

retailleau.name: Bruno Retailleau
retailleau.bio: The Vendée hardliner who went from Senate to Interior Minister in September 2024 under Barnier, then kept the job through the Bayrou government before leaving when Lecornu took over. He won the LR presidency in December 2025 with 74.64% of the vote against Wauquiez, then on February 12, 2026, officially declared his candidacy for the 2027 presidential election, pledging to be 'the President of order, justice and French pride.' As Interior Minister, he cracked down on immigration (issuing a restrictive circular on regularization in January 2025) and drug trafficking. His announcement came as polls show him as the most popular right-wing candidate among conservative voters, ahead of Wauquiez. The mild-mannered Catholic conservative who talks about 'restoring authority' while looking like a provincial accountant—the gap between his tough rhetoric and librarian appearance is pure meme gold.
retailleau.meme_summary: The grumpy conservative uncle who thinks France peaked in 1950 and wants to bring back 'traditional values' through authoritarian security measures.
retailleau.best_takedown_angle: Mock his outdated worldview and Vendée parochialism—he's a provincial politician pretending to understand modern France. His tough-guy act on security while looking like a mild-mannered accountant.
retailleau.combo_partners: Darmanin (dueling Interior Ministers), Wauquiez (conservative tag team), Le Pen (who wore the security hardliner pants first?)
retailleau.target_audience: Traditional conservatives, Catholic voters, rural France, security-focused right

valls.name: Manuel Valls
valls.bio: The political zombie who simply will not stay dead—after his humiliating Barcelona mayoral campaign (2018-2019), failed 2022 legislative run, and years in the political wilderness, Valls made a jaw-dropping comeback as Overseas Minister in Bayrou's December 2024 government, becoming the third-ranking member of the cabinet. He was thrust into managing the New Caledonia crisis before losing his ministerial position when Bayrou's government fell. In 2026, he popped up again in Barcelona, launching the Emet-Verdad foundation to fight antisemitism in Catalonia while still maintaining ties to French politics. The Franco-Spanish chameleon who keeps reinventing himself across two countries—from socialist PM to Macronist minister to anti-antisemitism activist—proving that the only thing more resilient than French bureaucracy is Manuel Valls's ambition.
valls.meme_summary: The politician who won't take a hint—like a bad reality show contestant who keeps getting voted off but keeps returning for 'All Stars' seasons.
valls.best_takedown_angle: Mock his desperation to stay relevant and his failed Barcelona adventure. He's the guy who burned every bridge and now begs for scraps at Macron's table.
valls.combo_partners: Macron (two former PMs, one desperate), Hollande (the mentor who wishes he'd disappear), Zemmour (two security hardliners with failed presidential bids)
valls.target_audience: Anti-Valls left, political schadenfreude enthusiasts, anti-establishment voters

barrot.name: Jean-Noël Barrot
barrot.role: Ministre de l'Europe et des Affaires étrangères
barrot.bio: Low-profile MoDem diplomat who climbed to Foreign Minister in the Barnier government (2024), then kept the role through Bayrou and Lecornu—making him one of France's longest-serving recent foreign ministers by default given the government chaos. Son of Jacques Barrot, former European Commissioner. Known as one of the 'polytraumatisés de Laurent Wauquiez.' Now handling France's response to Trump's return and the ongoing Ukraine war while maintaining French diplomatic presence in the Middle East. A competent centrist technocrat representing the pro-European establishment—the guy who keeps foreign policy running while everyone else fights over domestic politics.
barrot.meme_summary: The most boring Foreign Minister in recent memory—so forgettable that even diplomats at summits forget his name midway through handshakes.
barrot.best_takedown_angle: Mock his total lack of presence and personality—he's the perfect symbol of technocratic mediocrity. A Foreign Minister nobody can name.
barrot.combo_partners: Macron (the invisible Foreign Minister serving the Jupiterian President), Bayrou (MoDem's dynamic duo of boring)
barrot.target_audience: Euroskeptics, anti-centrist voters, nationalists frustrated with EU subservience

pannier_runacher.name: Agnès Pannier-Runacher
pannier_runacher.role: Ministre de l'Environnement, de la Biodiversité, des Forêts, de la Mer et de la Pêche
pannier_runacher.bio: Energy Minister who has become a central figure in France's energy transition debates, navigating the balance between nuclear dominance and renewable targets while keeping electricity prices manageable. Served under multiple PMs since 2024, representing the technocratic wing of Renaissance. Previously a Versailles parliamentarian who moved up through the Macron government ranks. A competent minister handling one of France's most sensitive portfolios—the energy file that affects every French household's electricity bill and industrial competitiveness.
pannier_runacher.meme_summary: The corporate environmentalist who thinks saving the planet means investing in nuclear stocks—climate action through Excel spreadsheets.
pannier_runacher.archetype: The corporate climate minister / Green capitalist
pannier_runacher.best_takedown_angle: Mock her corporate background and technocratic approach to climate—she's a banker pretending to care about nature while promoting nuclear power plants.
visual_gags: Posing next to nuclear cooling towers
Pointing at wind turbines like she's selling them
Corporate presentation pose at climate events
catchphrases: Le nucléaire est une solution bas-carbone
pannier_runacher.combo_partners: Macron (two former bankers saving the planet through capitalism), Hidalgo (dueling environmental approaches)
pannier_runacher.target_audience: Anti-nuclear activists, degrowth advocates, environmentalists skeptical of corporate solutions

dati.name: Rachida Dati
dati.bio: The first woman of North African heritage to hold a major French government post, Dati made a surprise comeback as Culture Minister in January 2024 under Gabriel Attal and kept the role through the Barnier, Bayrou and Lecornu governments. But her real ambition is the Paris mayor's race in March 2026—she's running as the LR candidate to replace Anne Hidalgo, campaigning on 'security, cleanliness, and fiscal responsibility.' While still serving as Culture Minister in early 2026, she announced she'd resign before the election. Her campaign takes inspiration from New York politics and targets eastern Paris voters who 'feel abandoned by the left.' The Sarkozy-era star who disappeared from the political map for a decade only to reinvent herself as both Culture Minister and mayoral hopeful—simultaneously—is either a political genius or a walking conflict of interest.
dati.meme_summary: The reality TV star of French politics—always dressed to the nines, always in some drama, and somehow still in government after countless controversies.
dati.best_takedown_angle: Mock her attention-seeking behavior and constant scandals—she treats politics like a fashion show mixed with a courtroom drama. The culture minister who brings more drama than culture.
catchphrases: Je ne suis pas une femme à prendre avec des pincettes
Je ne me laisse pas faire
dati.combo_partners: Sarkozy (the controversial duo returns), Zemmour (two media magnets who love attention), Macron (elitist culture vs populist culture clash)
dati.target_audience: Anti-elitist voters, culture war participants, anyone exhausted by political theatrics

le_maire.name: Bruno Le Maire
le_maire.bio: The longest-serving Economy Minister in recent French history (2017-2024) who finally left government in September 2024 after serving under three presidents. Briefly served as Armed Forces Minister in October 2025 under Lecornu before joining consulting firm Macro Advisory Partners. Was notably mentioned in the Epstein files revelation - reportedly met Jeffrey Epstein at his house, though no wrongdoing alleged. The man who 'managed Bercy' for seven years, now cashing in on his government experience as a private sector consultant.
le_maire.meme_summary: The philosophy student who became Defense Minister—still writing novels nobody reads while dreaming of the Élysée Palace.
le_maire.best_takedown_angle: Mock his pretentious intellectualism and failed literary career—he's a minister playing soldier while writing bad fiction. The ultimate 'too smart for politics' cliché.
visual_gags: Staring into distance at military bases
catchphrases: Nous devons penser le monde autrement
le_maire.combo_partners: Macron (two intellectuals who think they know best), Lecornu (Defense Minister duo planning... something), Philippe (the two 2027 hopefuls)
le_maire.target_audience: Anti-elitist voters, euroskeptics, anyone tired of pretentious politicians

panot.name: Mathilde Panot
panot.role: Présidente du groupe LFI à l'Assemblée nationale, Députée du Val-de-Marne
panot.bio: Leader of La France Insoumise in the National Assembly since 2022, making her the face of the left-wing opposition to Macron. In February 2026, she found herself at the center of a political storm when her assistant was accused involvement in the death of Quentin Deranque in Lyon - a militant identitaire who died after a confrontation. The case sparked heated debates about LFI's relationship with radical left groups and whether the party should expel Raphaël Arnault (the LFI deputy involved). Panot defended her party against calls to distance from 'violent' elements while the opposition demanded accountability.
panot.meme_summary: The attack dog of LFI—always screaming, always outraged, always ready to turn any debate into a revolutionary tribunal. Mélenchon's mini-me with maximum volume.
panot.best_takedown_angle: Mock her constant outrage and theatrical speeches—she's permanently at level 11 on the anger dial. The woman who thinks every budget debate is the storming of the Bastille.
catchphrases: Le peuple ne vous pardonnera pas
panot.combo_partners: Mélenchon (master and apprentice), Braun-Pivet (the clash of Assembly queens), Borne (49.3 vs permanent outrage)
panot.target_audience: Right-wing voters, centrists exhausted by LFI theatrics, political humor fans

boyard.name: Louis Boyard
boyard.role: Député LFI, Vice-président de la commission d'enquête TikTok
boyard.bio: Young LFI deputy (elected 2022) who became a TikTok star with 1M+ followers, known for viral explainers on politics. In 2024, he had a viral feud with Cyril Hanouna on TPMP where he tried to hide a luxury watch, leading to accusations of hypocrisy - a left-wing MP showcasing expensive bling while criticizing the wealthy. The incident became a meme about 'luxury socialism.' Represents the new generation of social media-savvy left-wing politicians balancing populism with influencer culture.
boyard.meme_summary: The Gen-Z deputy who treats the Assemblée nationale like a TikTok set—always looking for the next viral moment between rap clips and parliamentary pranks.
boyard.best_takedown_angle: Mock his lack of qualifications beyond social media fame—a former drug dealer turned TikTok politician. He treats parliament like a content creation studio.
catchphrases: La jeunesse ne se laissera pas faire
boyard.combo_partners: Bardella (Gen-Z political rivals), Panot (young LFI tag team), Hanouna (the eternal nemesis)
boyard.target_audience: Boomers shocked by Gen-Z politics, right-wing media, political traditionalists

delogu.name: Sébastien Delogu
delogu.role: Député LFI, Candidat à la mairie de Marseille 2026
delogu.bio: LFI deputy from Marseille elected in 2022 who became a viral sensation in May 2024 when he brandished a Palestinian flag in the National Assembly hemicycle—earning a 15-day suspension and instant hero status among pro-Palestinian voters. He also kissed the Algerian flag at a Gaza solidarity rally in June 2024. In February 2026, he's accused of having SMS exchanges with a drug trafficker (which he calls 'slander,' claiming the 'packages' were furniture). Now running for mayor of Marseille in 2026, campaigning against the 'corrupt system' of incumbent mayor Benoît Payan. The provocateur who turned Palestine solidarity into a political brand and Marseille street credibility into votes.
delogu.meme_summary: The Marseille street fighter who brings the city's chaos into the Assemblée—every controversy around him somehow involves drugs, gangs, or shouting matches.
delogu.best_takedown_angle: Mock his Marseille gangster image—the deputy who can't escape drug trafficking headlines even when he's innocent. Every political story about him reads like a Netflix crime series.
delogu.combo_partners: Mélenchon (LFI's Marseille operation), Dati (both facing legal troubles during campaigns), Darmanin (law and order vs street politics)
delogu.target_audience: Anti-LFI right, Marseille political watchers, crime drama fans

bilongo.name: Carlos Martens Bilongo
bilongo.bio: LFI deputy from Val-d'Oise elected in 2022, known for the shocking November 2022 incident when RN deputy Grégoire de Fournas shouted what was interpreted as 'back to Africa' during Bilongo's question about a migrant rescue ship—sparking a national racism debate and de Fournas's 15-day suspension. Part of the NFP coalition re-elected in 2024. Represents the diverse outer suburbs and has spoken out on colonial history and police violence. The LFI deputy whose very existence in the Assembly triggers far-right responses that become national scandals.
bilongo.meme_summary: The controversy magnet who went from victim of parliamentary racism to generating his own scandals—a polémique machine who can't stay out of the headlines for more than a week.
bilongo.best_takedown_angle: The irony of going from racism victim to generating his own offensive controversies. A politician who generates more headlines for gaffes than legislation.
bilongo.combo_partners: Fournas (the eternal linked pair), Dati (his sexist comments target), Bertrand (northern France defender)
bilongo.target_audience: Political watchers, controversy followers, anti-LFI voters

fournas.name: Grégoire de Fournas
fournas.bio: RN deputy from Gironde who became nationally infamous in November 2022 when he shouted what was widely interpreted as 'back to Africa' at Black LFI deputy Carlos Martens Bilongo during a debate on migrants—earning a 15-day suspension from the Assembly. He claimed he was referring to the migrants, not Bilongo, but the damage was done and the incident became a defining moment in the racism debate. Re-elected in 2024 as part of the RN wave. The deputy who gave the internet exactly what it wanted from RN: an unfiltered moment that confirmed every suspicion about the party's base.
fournas.meme_summary: The deputy who got kicked out of parliament for racism and celebrated by selling his wine on Twitter—the perfect embodiment of 'tone deaf politician.'
fournas.best_takedown_angle: The absolute absurdity of shouting racist slurs in parliament and then using the resulting fame to sell your vineyard wine on Twitter. Peak political shamelessness.
visual_gags: Wine bottle with 'Retourne en Afrique' label
Wine tasting at home during suspension
fournas.combo_partners: Bilongo (the eternal linked pair), Le Pen (RN racism problems), Bardella (RN image damage)
fournas.target_audience: Anti-RN voters, anti-racism activists, wine lovers with irony

chikirou.name: Sophia Chikirou
chikirou.role: Députée LFI, Candidate à la mairie de Paris 2026
chikirou.bio: LFI's communications chief and close Mélenchon ally who was indicted in September 2024 for aggravated fraud and misuse of corporate assets related to the 2017 presidential campaign - authorities allege her company Mediascop overbilled for services. Despite the indictment, she's running for mayor of Paris in 2026, presenting herself as the 'populist' alternative to both the right and the PS. The irony of a left-wing firebrand accused of financial wrongdoing hasn't been lost on critics. She's managed to keep her LFI position despite the legal troubles, showing once again thatMélenchon's inner circle protects its own.
chikirou.meme_summary: LFI's shadowy fixer who went from running Mélenchon's media empire to running for Paris mayor while dodging fraud charges—the puppet master who became the puppet.
chikirou.best_takedown_angle: Mock her dual role as LFI's propaganda chief and accused fraudster—she founded a left-wing media outlet then allegedly embezzled from it. Running for mayor while facing trial is peak LFI audacity.
visual_gags: Campaign poster next to court summons
catchphrases: Le Média est un projet collectif
chikirou.combo_partners: Mélenchon (the power couple), Dati (rival Paris mayoral candidate both in legal trouble), Hidalgo (fighting for Paris)
chikirou.target_audience: Anti-LFI voters, media watchers, Paris politics followers

ciotti.name: Éric Ciotti
ciotti.role: Député des Alpes-Maritimes, Président de l'UDR
ciotti.bio: The man who detonated a bomb inside his own party: as LR president, he stunned France in June 2024 by unilaterally announcing an alliance with Le Pen's RN for the snap legislative elections, splitting LR in two. Expelled from the party (which he contested in court), he formed the UDR (Union des Droites pour la République) in alliance with RN. In February 2026, his UDR-RN candidate won a by-election in Haute-Savoie, validating his 'union of the right' strategy. Now running for mayor of Nice against his former ally Christian Estrosi, Ciotti is proving that his LR-RN alliance is electorally viable while the traditional LR crumbles. Whether he's a traitor or a prophet depends on which side of the right you're on—but the electoral results are starting to suggest he may have been ahead of the curve.
ciotti.meme_summary: The man who blew up the French right by switching sides mid-election—like a football captain joining the rival team at halftime. Peak political betrayal.
ciotti.best_takedown_angle: Mock his spectacular betrayal of his own party—he was literally LR's president when he allied with the RN. The man who destroyed the Gaullist right from the inside.
visual_gags: Shaking hands with Le Pen while LR burns
catchphrases: Le vrai courage c'est de voir la réalité
ciotti.combo_partners: Le Pen (the unlikely alliance), Wauquiez (the betrayed rival), Bardella (new best friends), Retailleau (LR vs UDR civil war)
ciotti.target_audience: LR loyalists (betrayal angle), anti-RN voters (normalization angle), political drama fans
memes: Party president who destroyed his party

braun_pivet.name: Yaël Braun-Pivet
braun_pivet.bio: The President of the National Assembly since June 2022, re-elected in July 2024 after the snap elections - making her one of the most powerful women in French politics. A Macron loyalist from the Yvelines constituency, she's presided over a deeply divided parliament with skill. In February 2026, she made headlines by blocking access to the Palais Bourbon for Jacques-Élie Favrot (linked to the Lyon fatal confrontation) and called on LFI to expel Raphaël Arnault. She also sparked controversy by opposing a parliamentary inquiry into the French side of the Epstein affair. Represents the institutional center holding together a fractured political system.
braun_pivet.meme_summary: The referee of France's most chaotic parliament in decades—trying to maintain order while deputies literally scream at each other.
braun_pivet.best_takedown_angle: Mock the chaos of the Assembly under her watch—every session looks like a school cafeteria food fight. She's supposed to maintain dignity but presides over political WWE.
visual_gags: Banging the gavel while nobody listens
Trying to call order in a room of screaming deputies
catchphrases: Mes chers collègues, un peu de calme
braun_pivet.combo_partners: Larcher (the two chamber presidents), Macron (his Assembly president managing his mess), Mélenchon (the biggest troublemaker she has to manage)
braun_pivet.target_audience: Parliamentary drama fans, political chaos enthusiasts, anyone who watches Assembly sessions for entertainment

lepen.name: Marine Le Pen
lepen.role: Cheffe de file RN / Candidate présidentielle 2027
lepen.bio: The three-time presidential candidate who was on the verge of finally winning it all—polls had her leading for 2027—until a Paris court convicted her on March 31, 2025 of embezzling EU Parliament funds (fake assistant jobs from 2004-2016), sentencing her to four years (two suspended) and a FIVE-YEAR BAN from holding public office. Her appeal trial ran from January 13 to February 12, 2026, with the ruling set for July 7, 2026. She changed defense strategy, admitting 'mistakes' while blaming the EU Parliament for not alerting her—essentially blaming the victim. If the ban is upheld, she'll withdraw and anoint Jordan Bardella as the RN's 2027 candidate. She played kingmaker during France's political chaos, helping topple Barnier's government in December 2024 while keeping her RN deputies in line. The woman who spent 50 years trying to make the far-right respectable may see her dream die in a courtroom.
lepen.meme_summary: France's political final boss who keeps losing presidential elections but somehow gets stronger each time—now with added courtroom drama.
lepen.best_takedown_angle: For right-wing content, she's more ally than target—but mock her repeated presidential failures and legal troubles. The eternal 'almost president' who can't close the deal.
visual_gags: Courtroom entrance like a movie villain
lepen.combo_partners: Macron (the eternal duel), Bardella (mentor and heir), Mélenchon (two populists screaming past each other), Zemmour (the far-right civil war)
lepen.target_audience: RN supporters (positive memes), centrists and left (negative memes), political drama fans

bardella.name: Jordan Bardella
bardella.bio: The 30-year-old TikTok-savvy RN president who led the party to historic gains in the 2024 European and legislative elections, positioning himself as Marine Le Pen's natural successor. Since Le Pen's March 2025 conviction and 5-year office ban, Bardella has been the de facto RN presidential candidate for 2027, leading polls against all comers. He's been distancing the party from its pro-Russia past—describing Russia as a 'threat' to France and Europe, in a notable split with Le Pen's more accommodating stance. The internal fracture over Russia represents a generational shift in the party. Bardella's challenge is maintaining RN's momentum while Le Pen's fate remains uncertain until the July 2026 appeal ruling. The Gen-Z far-right leader who communicates via Instagram Reels and looks like he stepped out of a fashion magazine—the normalization of the far-right packaged for the smartphone era.
bardella.meme_summary: The TikTok nationalist who looks like a student council president but leads France's largest party—Gen Z's answer to far-right politics.
bardella.archetype: The young rising star / Smooth operator
bardella.best_takedown_angle: For right-wing content, he's more of a hero—but mock his polished image vs substance gap. He's all vibes and no policy details, the Instagram filter of French politics.
visual_gags: Perfect hair never out of place
Looking too young for the job
catchphrases: Le changement c'est maintenant... pour de vrai
bardella.combo_partners: Le Pen (the mentor-heir dynamic), Macron (young vs young showdown), Mélenchon (generational clash), Bergé (TV debate sparring partner)
bardella.target_audience: Young right-wing voters, TikTok political audience, Gen Z nationalists, anti-immigration youth

melenchon.name: Jean-Luc Mélenchon
melenchon.bio: The firebrand leader of La France Insoumise who came third in both 2017 and 2022 presidential elections (22% in 2022). Since October 2023, he's made the Israel-Gaza war his central issue, calling it 'genocide' and supporting South Africa's ICJ case. This stance has alienated allies but consolidated his base in Muslim-majority suburbs. His LFI party got 9.89% in the 2024 EU elections (up from 6.3% in 2019) and performed well in the 2024 snap legislative elections as part of the NFP coalition. In February 2026, he's making an 'offer' to communists for a presidential alliance while refusing to participate in the left-wing primary process backed by Ruffin. The LFI leader embroiled in a massive controversy after the death of Quentin Deranque in Lyon involving LFI members. He famously said he's 'more comfortable singing the Marseillaise when thinking about Gaza'—a quote that simultaneously delights his base and horrifies his critics. The undisputed king of left-wing populism who refuses to share the throne with anyone.
melenchon.meme_summary: The screaming grandpa of French politics who treats every press conference like a Shakespearean monologue—maximum drama, minimum chill.
melenchon.best_takedown_angle: Mock his theatrical outbursts, ego, and hypocrisy—a millionaire socialist who vacations in Latin America while preaching about the working class. His ego is bigger than his voter base.
visual_gags: Cuba vacation photos vs poverty speeches
melenchon.combo_partners: Macron (ego vs ego, unstoppable force vs immovable object), Le Pen (two populists, opposite directions), Roussel (communist BBQ vs revolutionary theater)
melenchon.target_audience: Right-wing memers (mockery), anti-LFI crowd, political drama fans, centrists who find him terrifying

ruffin.name: François Ruffin
ruffin.bio: The documentary filmmaker turned politician who made 'Merci Patron!' before breaking spectacularly with LFI in 2024 over strategic disagreements with Mélenchon. In January 2026, he officially announced his presidential candidacy through the 'left-wing primary' process, positioning himself as the authentic working-class left alternative to both Mélenchon's radical posturing and PS's moderate sellout. He famously said he'd 'pay his electricity bills like de Gaulle' and wants to be 'the first president on the Smic.' Running on a platform of radical democratic renewal and anti-oligarchic populism, he's trying to carve out a Third Way between LFI and PS for 2027.
ruffin.meme_summary: The documentary guy who accidentally became a politician and then fell out with Mélenchon—France's Michael Moore but with a better accent and worse haircut.
ruffin.best_takedown_angle: Mock his 'man of the people' act—he's a successful filmmaker playing working class. The LFI rebel who couldn't even get along with fellow rebels.
visual_gags: Camera in hand even in parliament
Factory visit outfit that's too clean
catchphrases: Je suis un homme du peuple
ruffin.combo_partners: Mélenchon (the bitter breakup), Macron (the elite vs the people narrative), Poutou (two working-class heroes comparing authenticity)
ruffin.target_audience: Anti-left memers, LFI internal drama fans, anyone mocking 'champagne socialism'

wauquiez.name: Laurent Wauquiez
wauquiez.bio: The ambitious LR politician who served as party president (2017-2019), then ran to reclaim the presidency in 2025 but lost to Bruno Retailleau (25.36% vs 74.64%). Now leading the 'Droite Républicaine' group in the National Assembly, he's been calling for a single right-wing presidential candidate and a 'primary of the right from Darmanin to Sarah Knafo.' In February 2026, after Retailleau declared his candidacy, Wauquiez said he 'obviously' considers running himself, calling for right-wing unity. Known as a 'polytraumatiser' of former allies (Barrot called himself one of the 'polytraumatisés de Wauquiez'), he embodies the calculating ambitious politician who keeps failing upward. The eternal also-ran of the French right who's always one step behind whoever just leapfrogged him.
wauquiez.meme_summary: The skiing conservative who runs his party like a mountain resort—exclusive, expensive, and increasingly empty.
wauquiez.best_takedown_angle: Mock his elitist background pretending to be a man of the people, and LR's declining relevance. He's the president of a party that's bleeding voters to both Macron and Le Pen.
visual_gags: Skiing photo ops in the Alps
Looking enviously at Le Pen's poll numbers
wauquiez.combo_partners: Macron (the centrist who stole his voters), Le Pen (the populist who stole the rest), Retailleau (conservative tag team), Sarkozy (the ghost of LR past)
wauquiez.target_audience: Traditional right-wing voters, LR loyalists, anti-Macron conservatives

philippe.name: Édouard Philippe
philippe.role: Maire du Havre / Candidat à la présidentielle 2027
philippe.bio: Former Prime Minister (2017-2020) and Mayor of Le Havre who formally announced his 2027 presidential candidacy on September 3, 2024—making him the first official candidate. Polling as one of France's most popular politicians, he's simultaneously running for re-election as mayor of Le Havre in March 2026, a dual strategy that critics call contradictory but he sees as proving his local credibility. His Horizons party positions itself as the centrist-right alternative to both Macron's dying Renaissance and the LR/RN right. He's been careful to distance himself from Macron's chaotic second term while benefiting from name recognition built during COVID-era governance. The calm, bald, no-drama politician who looks like the anti-Macron—competent without the ego, boring without the chaos—which might be exactly what France wants after years of political fireworks.
philippe.meme_summary: The smooth-talking ex-PM with the iconic beard who's patiently waiting for 2027 like a cat watching a mouse—France's most obvious presidential candidate in waiting.
philippe.best_takedown_angle: Mock his calculated ambiguity—he never commits to anything, always hedging for 2027. He's the political equivalent of a 'coming soon' page that's been up for 5 years.
visual_gags: Looking at Le Havre harbor like a ship captain
catchphrases: Je ne suis candidat à rien
philippe.combo_partners: Macron (the protégé who might betray the master), Le Maire (two 2027 hopefuls circling each other), Wauquiez (the right-wing rival), Darmanin (another 2027 contender)
philippe.target_audience: Political strategists, 2027 election watchers, moderate right voters, beard enthusiasts

zemmour.name: Éric Zemmour
zemmour.bio: The former journalist and far-right provocateur who founded Reconquête in 2021 and ran in 2022 (7% in first round). By 2024-2025, his party was in decline - multiple defections to RN after Ciotti's alliance, internal purges of dissenting members, and polls showing him stuck at 5-7% while Bardella dominates the far-right space. Still leads Reconquête and appears regularly on TV calling Mélenchon's LFI 'the party of the Great Replacement,' but increasingly looks like a one-man show with no institutional support. His 2026 strategy is to propose 'fusion of the right' with LR and RN in municipal elections - essentially admitting he can't win alone and needs to merge into larger parties.
zemmour.meme_summary: The TV pundit who decided arguing on television wasn't enough so he started a whole political party—France's answer to 'what if a Fox News host ran for president.'
zemmour.best_takedown_angle: Mock his transition from armchair pundit to failed politician—easier to criticize from a TV studio than to actually win elections. His party imploded almost as fast as it was created.
catchphrases: La France est en train de mourir
On ne peut plus rien dire
zemmour.combo_partners: Le Pen (the far-right rivalry), Macron (establishment vs insurgent), Mélenchon (two theatrical extremes), Darmanin (two security obsessives)
zemmour.target_audience: Identitarian right, culture war enthusiasts, anti-immigration hardliners, CNews viewers

berge.name: Aurore Bergé
berge.role: Ministre de l'Égalité entre les femmes et les hommes et de la Lutte contre les discriminations, Porte-parole du gouvernement
berge.bio: The minister for Equality and Anti-discrimination since 2025, formerly a LR member who defected to Macron's party. A rising star in the Macron camp, she's positioned herself as the voice of the 'central' politics, calling for a primary to unite 'the central block from Republicans to Renaissance.' In February 2026, she launched 'Talents de France' initiative to fight discrimination in employment. Also made headlines denouncing the Lyon fatal confrontation involving LFI, pointing to the party's 'responsibility' for political violence. A potential 2027 candidate if the center can unite - though she polls at just 5%.
berge.meme_summary: The government's attack dog who'll defend literally anything on TV with a straight face—the ultimate 'this is fine' spokesperson.
berge.best_takedown_angle: Mock her role as professional spin doctor—she's paid to say everything is great while France burns. The Equality Minister who represents the most unequal government coalition.
visual_gags: Defending absurd positions on TV without blinking
Spinning bad news at press conferences
catchphrases: Je ne laisserai personne dire que...
berge.combo_partners: Macron (the messenger and the message), Dati (two fierce women in cabinet), Mélenchon (ultimate political sparring partner on TV)
berge.target_audience: Anti-government viewers, media critics, culture war participants

roussel.name: Fabien Roussel
roussel.role: Secrétaire national du Parti Communiste Français
roussel.bio: The PCF leader since 2018 who ran in 2022 (2.3%) and is preparing to run again in 2027. Lost his National Assembly seat in the 2024 snap elections, defeated by RN in his Nord constituency. Now campaigning for mayor of Saint-Amand-les-Eaux while simultaneously positioning for another presidential run, arguing the PCF needs its own candidate rather than joining a left coalition. The perennial communist candidate who keeps the Marxist flame alive in French politics despite his party's declining relevance.
roussel.meme_summary: The communist who loves BBQ more than revolution—the only French politician who'd rather discuss steak than class struggle.
roussel.best_takedown_angle: Mock the absurdity of a communist who sounds like a food blogger. He represents a party with Soviet heritage but campaigns on the quality of French sausages.
visual_gags: Grilling sausages at a party rally
Wine glass in hand at communist events
Food critic pose at political events
catchphrases: Un bon vin, une bonne viande, un bon fromage
Le communisme c'est la joie de vivre
roussel.combo_partners: Mélenchon (the angry communist vs the happy communist), Macron (the champagne president vs the BBQ communist), Arthaud (real communists vs vibes communists)
roussel.target_audience: Foodies, political humor fans, anyone who enjoys the absurdity of a BBQ communist, right-wing mockery of the left

dupont_moretti.name: Éric Dupond-Moretti
dupont_moretti.bio: The famous criminal lawyer turned Justice Minister (2020-2024) who was Macron's 'bad cop' - known for aggressive courtroom style and public battles with judges. Left the government in 2024 after serving under three PMs (Castex, Borne, Attal). Since leaving, he's returned to what he does best: performing. In 2025, he launched a one-man show at the Théâtre Marigny called '36 ans de barre' (36 years at the bar), recounting his greatest courtroom victories. The 'Acquittor' who made justice political and entertaining, now monetizing his celebrity as a stage performer.
dupont_moretti.meme_summary: The defense lawyer who switched sides—like a bank robber becoming the bank manager. His courtroom theatrics made parliament look boring.
dupont_moretti.best_takedown_angle: Mock the absurdity of France's most famous defense lawyer running the justice system—and getting tried himself while doing it. Peak irony.
catchphrases: La justice n'est pas une machine
Je ne me laisse pas impressionner
dupont_moretti.combo_partners: Darmanin (the justice duo), Macron (the unexpected minister), Retailleau (law and order clash)
dupont_moretti.target_audience: Legal drama fans, political irony appreciators, courtroom enthusiasts

sarkozy.name: Nicolas Sarkozy
sarkozy.bio: The former president (2007-2012) who just can't stay out of prison. Convicted in the Bygmalion case (illegal campaign financing) in February 2024, sentenced to one year (six months suspended), served under bracelet electronic from February to May 2025. Then in September 2025, convicted in the Libyan financing case - five years prison for complicity in criminal association, incarcerated at La Santé prison in October 2025. He's now fighting to avoid wearing the bracelet again - his lawyers filed for 'confusion of sentences' in November 2025. The man who said he wanted to 'shield' the French from justice is now a convicted felon facing actual jail time. Still influential in LR through his loyalists, though most have distanced themselves.
sarkozy.meme_summary: The bling-bling president who went from the Élysée to the courthouse—still strutting around like he owns the place while wearing an ankle bracelet.
sarkozy.best_takedown_angle: Mock his legal troubles and undying arrogance. A convicted criminal who still acts like a kingmaker. The Kärcher president who got cleaned up by the justice system instead.
catchphrases: La France qui se lève tôt
Je vais vous dire une chose
sarkozy.combo_partners: Macron (two presidents, both hated), Dati (the Sarko-era reunion tour), Woerth (Bettencourt scandal buddies), Le Pen (the rivalry that defined an era)
sarkozy.target_audience: Anti-corruption voters, nostalgia memers, legal drama fans, anyone who remembers the bling-bling era

lescure.name: Roland Lescure
lescure.role: Ministre de l'Économie, des Finances et de la Souveraineté industrielle et numérique
lescure.bio: Renaissance deputy from Paris who served as Industry Minister and government spokesman under various Macron governments. A smooth communications operative who mastered the art of spinning unpopular policies. Represents the Paris Macronist establishment and remains active in the Assembly. The polished voice of the governing majority who explains why unpopular austerity measures are actually good for you—the political equivalent of a corporate PR department.
lescure.meme_summary: The guy who left Canada to manage France's economy like a hedge fund—because what could go wrong letting Wall Street types run a country?
lescure.best_takedown_angle: Mock his finance-bro background and disconnect from real economic struggles. He talks about 'sovereignty' while having spent years managing money in North America.
visual_gags: Pointing at economic charts going down
The 'trust me I'm from finance' smile
lescure.combo_partners: Macron (two finance bros running France), Montchalin (the budget couple), Le Maire (former vs current economy boss)
lescure.target_audience: Anti-globalists, small business owners, economic populists

vautrin.name: Catherine Vautrin
vautrin.role: Ministre du Travail, de la Santé, des Solidarités et des Familles
vautrin.bio: Horizons politician who served as Minister for Labour, Health and Solidarity in the Barnier government (2024). A former journalist turned center-right politician closely allied to Édouard Philippe. Handling France's health system crisis including hospital closures and GP shortages. Represents the moderate wing of the Macron coalition that's trying to hold the center together while extremes gain ground on both sides.
vautrin.meme_summary: The minister with the longest job title and the shortest patience—managing France's crumbling health system, labor disputes, AND family policy simultaneously.
vautrin.best_takedown_angle: Mock the absurdity of one person managing health, labor, social affairs AND families—it's a setup for failure. She's the designated fall guy for every social crisis.
visual_gags: Juggling hospital files, labor reports, and family brochures
The 'everything is fine' smile while chaos erupts
Reading her own impossibly long title
vautrin.combo_partners: Macron (the president who gives one minister four impossible jobs), Borne (two technocrats managing the unmanageable)
vautrin.target_audience: Healthcare workers, unions, families frustrated with social policy

genevard.name: Annie Genevard
genevard.role: Ministre de l'Agriculture et de la Souveraineté alimentaire
genevard.bio: The Agriculture Minister since 2024, handling one of France's most crisis-ridden sectors. A LR member who served as interim party president after Wauquiez's departure, then joined the Barnier government as Agriculture Minister and stayed through Bayrou and Lecornu. In 2026, she's battling to protect French farmers from the Mercosur deal and dealing with the collapse of France's agri-food trade surplus (from €3.9 billion to €200 million in one year). Also facing backlash from farmers over wolf attacks and from supermarkets over her 'chantage mortifère' comments about grocery negotiations. The moderate conservative stuck implementing policies she probably disagrees with.
genevard.meme_summary: The farmer's best friend in Paris who probably hasn't touched actual soil since the last Salon de l'Agriculture photo op.
genevard.best_takedown_angle: Mock the disconnect between her Paris ministerial life and actual farmers struggling to survive. She's a career politician cosplaying as a friend of agriculture.
visual_gags: Awkwardly petting a cow at Salon de l'Agriculture
Wearing wellies that look brand new
Holding wheat sheaves for photo ops
catchphrases: Nos agriculteurs sont le cœur de la France
Le monde rural ne doit pas être oublié
genevard.combo_partners: Retailleau (rural conservative duo), Macron (the Paris elite vs rural France contrast), Roussel (the BBQ communist vs the agriculture minister)
genevard.target_audience: Farmers, rural voters, agricultural unions, anyone frustrated with EU agricultural policy

montchalin.name: Amélie de Montchalin
montchalin.bio: Former minister who served in various roles under Macron including Ecological Transition Minister and European Affairs Minister. A competent technocrat from the moderate Macron wing who lost her National Assembly seat in the 2024 snap elections, becoming a casualty of Macron's dissolution gamble. Now repositioning in the center-right space, potentially as part of the 'central primary' discussion for 2027. Represents the moderate technocrats who rose with Macron and fell when his parliamentary majority collapsed.
montchalin.meme_summary: The budget minister trying to balance France's books with an HEC degree and a prayer—like hiring an accountant for a ship that's already sinking.
montchalin.best_takedown_angle: Mock the impossibility of her job and her elite background. An HEC-Harvard graduate trying to fix a deficit created by her own party's spending—the fox guarding the henhouse.
visual_gags: Staring at deficit numbers in horror
The forced optimism at budget presentations
Calculator in hand, tears in eyes
montchalin.combo_partners: Macron (the president who spends, the minister who counts), Lescure (economy and budget duo staring at the abyss), Le Maire (two budget hawks who never balanced anything)
montchalin.target_audience: Fiscal conservatives, taxpayers, anyone angry about government spending

moutchou.name: Naïma Moutchou
moutchou.role: Ministre de la Transformation et de l'Action publiques, de l'Intelligence artificielle et du Numérique
moutchou.bio: Horizons deputy from Val-d'Oise who has been active in justice and security debates in the Assembly. A moderate conservative allied to Édouard Philippe's Horizons party. Represents the suburban center-right that bridges Macronism and traditional conservatism. Known for pragmatic policy positions on immigration and security that try to split the difference between the Macron camp and LR.
moutchou.meme_summary: France's AI minister who probably asks ChatGPT how to do her job—the living embodiment of 'we added AI to the job title to sound modern.'
moutchou.best_takedown_angle: Mock the absurdity of France having an 'AI Minister' while the country's public services still run on fax machines. The title is longer than any actual achievement.
visual_gags: Posing with robots at tech fairs
The impossibly long job title on her door
catchphrases: L'intelligence artificielle au service des Français
moutchou.combo_partners: Macron (the 'Start-up Nation' president and his AI minister), Lescure (tech sovereignty double act), Barrot (two ministers nobody can name)
moutchou.target_audience: Tech skeptics, anti-AI crowd, anyone who's dealt with French bureaucracy online

tabarot.name: Philippe Tabarot
tabarot.bio: LR senator from Alpes-Maritimes who served as Transport Minister in the Lecornu government. A representative of the Mediterranean right from the Nice political establishment. Handling France's transport infrastructure challenges including rail modernization and urban mobility. The kind of competent but low-profile minister who keeps the trains running (literally) while the political drama plays out above.
tabarot.meme_summary: The Transport Minister whose trains are always late—the perfect scapegoat for every cancelled SNCF service and every traffic jam on the autoroute.
tabarot.best_takedown_angle: Mock the eternal failure of French transport under any minister. Every delayed train, every metro breakdown, every gridlocked highway is his problem now.
visual_gags: Standing on an empty train platform
Looking at his watch while trains are cancelled
Riding a bike because SNCF is on strike
The 'it's not my fault' shrug at press conferences
tabarot.combo_partners: Hidalgo (the bike vs car war), Macron (the president promises infrastructure, minister delivers delays), SNCF (the eternal enemy)
tabarot.target_audience: Commuters, anyone who's ever waited for a late SNCF train, car enthusiasts

ferrari.name: Marina Ferrari
ferrari.role: Ministre des Sports, de la Jeunesse et de la Vie associative
ferrari.bio: MoDem politician from Savoie who served as Secretary of State for Digital Affairs and has been active in tech policy debates. Represents the Alpine center-right that bridges rural France with the Macronist establishment. Part of the Bayrou wing of the governing coalition that provides steady moderate voices in an increasingly polarized political landscape.
ferrari.meme_summary: A Sports Minister named Ferrari who moves at the speed of a Twingo—the name writes the jokes itself.
ferrari.best_takedown_angle: Mock the name-role disconnect (Ferrari = fast, politics = slow) and the post-Olympic letdown. Also the classic 'unknown politician given a ministry' template.
visual_gags: Ferrari logo juxtaposed with slow bureaucracy
Trying to look athletic at sporting events
catchphrases: Le sport est un vecteur de cohésion sociale
ferrari.combo_partners: Macron (Olympic legacy pair), Hidalgo (Paris sports infrastructure debate), Bardella (youth appeal contest)
ferrari.target_audience: Sports fans, post-Olympic cynics, youth policy critics

lefevre.name: Mathieu Lefèvre
lefevre.role: Ministre des Relations avec le Parlement
lefevre.bio: Renaissance politician who became Minister for Ecological Transition in the second Lecornu government (October 2025). In February 2026, he co-announced with Agriculture Minister Genevard new measures allowing farmers to shoot wolves attacking unprotected livestock—a controversial policy balancing rural interests against environmental protection. Represents the pragmatic technocratic wing of Renaissance handling the difficult intersection of agriculture and ecology.
lefevre.meme_summary: The guy whose job is to convince parliament not to overthrow the government—like a hostage negotiator but with worse odds.
lefevre.best_takedown_angle: Mock the impossibility of his job—negotiating with a parliament that hates the government. He's the designated punching bag for every parliamentary ambush.
visual_gags: Carrying olive branches into hostile territory
lefevre.combo_partners: Macron (the president who needs parliament but can't get it), Borne (two people who know the pain of 49.3), Braun-Pivet (Assembly president duo)
lefevre.target_audience: Political junkies, parliamentary procedure nerds, anti-government crowd

woerth.name: Eric Woerth
woerth.role: Ministre de l'Aménagement du territoire et de la Décentralisation
woerth.bio: LR veteran and former Budget Minister (2007-2010) under Sarkozy, later president of the National Assembly's Finance Committee. Defected from LR to join Macron's Renaissance in 2022, causing shockwaves in the conservative camp. Has been navigating the increasingly crowded center-right space as both LR and Renaissance compete for the same voters. A Gaullist turned Macronist whose political journey mirrors the broader collapse of the traditional right-left divide in French politics.
woerth.meme_summary: The Bettencourt scandal survivor who switched parties like changing socks—proof that in French politics, scandals are just career speedbumps.
woerth.best_takedown_angle: Mock his scandal-ridden past and party-switching opportunism. The Bettencourt affair should have ended his career, but here he is, still in government.
woerth.combo_partners: Sarkozy (the old Sarkozy gang), Macron (the president who recycles everyone), Darmanin (two party-hoppers in the same government)
woerth.target_audience: Anti-corruption voters, anyone who remembers the Bettencourt affair, political cynics

aubry.name: Manon Aubry
aubry.role: Eurodéputée LFI, Co-présidente groupe gauche au Parlement européen
aubry.bio: The LFI EU Parliament member and former Oxfam staffer who, with Manon Aubry, co-leads the Left group in EU Parliament. In the 2024 European elections, she ran in the Seine-Saint-Denis constituency and got nearly 60% - demonstrating LFI's dominance in working-class areas. Active in municipal politics, campaigning for LFI candidates in Perpignan and Bordeaux. Known for fiery speeches against EU trade deals (celebrated the Parliament's legal challenge to Mercosur in January 2026) and calling Trump's return a threat to European sovereignty. The young progressive face of French leftism in Europe.
aubry.meme_summary: The NGO activist who went from protesting outside Parliament to running it—still talks like she's at a protest rally, just with better seating.
aubry.archetype: The young activist / European left champion
aubry.best_takedown_angle: Mock her transition from NGO world to political establishment—she's become the very institutional figure she used to protest against. Oxfam to EU Parliament is quite the career pivot.
visual_gags: Protest sign in one hand, EU Parliament badge in the other
aubry.combo_partners: Mélenchon (the LFI double act), Macron (everything she opposes), Bardella (youth vs youth across the aisle)
aubry.target_audience: Right-wing voters mocking left activism, anti-EU crowd, fiscal policy critics

faure.name: Olivier Faure
faure.bio: The PS First Secretary since 2018 who somehow keeps winning internal party battles despite the PS's national decline. Won re-election at the Nancy congress in 2025 against Nicolas Mayer-Rossignol, though with a narrow majority. His strategy of 'accommodation' with the Macron government - refusing to censure Bayrou in February 2025 in exchange for budget concessions - made him a controversial figure within his own party. The 'Faure sure' meme (ironic play on Macron's 'for sure') mocks his optimistic political spin. Now positioning the PS for 2027 while the left remains fragmented between PS, LFI, and Place publique.
faure.meme_summary: The leader of a party that went from running France to running on fumes—the political equivalent of a Nokia phone in the iPhone era.
faure.archetype: The party manager / Left coalition builder
faure.best_takedown_angle: Mock the PS's total irrelevance under his leadership. He runs a party that got 1.75% in the 2022 presidential election—that's not a political party, that's a rounding error.
visual_gags: Counting remaining party members on one hand
catchphrases: Nous sommes la gauche de gouvernement
faure.combo_partners: Mélenchon (the guy who ate his voters), Hidalgo (the 1.75% presidential candidate), Macron (the centrist who stole the rest)
faure.target_audience: Political historians, nostalgia voters, anyone who enjoys watching political decline

hidalgo.name: Anne Hidalgo
hidalgo.bio: The Paris Mayor since 2014 who got a humiliating 1.75% in the 2022 presidential election - the worst result for a Socialist candidate in the party's history. Despite the disaster, she finished her second term and announced she won't run for a third in 2026, paving the way for a competitive succession battle (with Dati, Chikirou, and others vying for the job). Known for controversial policies (pedestrianizing the Seine, 2024 Olympics legacy) and a stubborn refusal to admit mistakes. The Spanish-born mayor who transformed Paris but couldn't translate local success to national relevance - now limping toward the end of her mayoral tenure with her legacy mixed at best.
hidalgo.meme_summary: The bike lane tyrant who turned Paris into a construction zone, promised to swim in the Seine, and got fewer presidential votes than a YouTube prankster would.
hidalgo.archetype: The Paris bubble queen / Bike lane dictator
hidalgo.best_takedown_angle: Mock her Paris-centric worldview, the endless construction chaos, and the 1.75% presidential disaster. The Seine swim promise is an eternal meme goldmine.
visual_gags: Riding a bike through traffic chaos
Banning cars while stuck in traffic
catchphrases: La voiture n'a plus sa place
hidalgo.combo_partners: Macron (two Parisian elites), Faure (the PS disaster duo), Tabarot (transport minister vs anti-car mayor), Mélenchon (left-wing infighting)
hidalgo.target_audience: Parisian drivers, suburban commuters, anti-bike crowd, anyone who remembers the Seine swim promise

dupont_aignan.name: Nicolas Dupont-Aignan
dupont_aignan.bio: The perennial sovereigntist who founded Debout la France in 2008 and runs for president every cycle (4.7% in 2017, 2.1% in 2022). Lost his National Assembly seat representing Essonne's 8th constituency in 2024 after holding it since 1997—a devastating blow to his political base. A Gaullist nationalist combining economic nationalism with anti-EU and anti-NATO positions. Endorsed Marine Le Pen in 2017's second round (becoming her hypothetical PM), then fell out with RN. Still planning to run in 2027 but increasingly squeezed between RN, LR, and Reconquête on the right with no institutional base left.
dupont_aignan.meme_summary: The political equivalent of that one guy who shows up to every party uninvited—always running, never winning, but absolutely convinced this is his year.
dupont_aignan.archetype: The Gaullist lone wolf / Eternal candidate
dupont_aignan.best_takedown_angle: Mock his total irrelevance and delusions of grandeur. He runs a party of one, gets 2% in elections, and still acts like he's the savior of France.
visual_gags: Solo photo at events where everyone else has crowds
catchphrases: Ni droite ni gauche, la France!
dupont_aignan.combo_partners: Le Pen (the awkward 2017 alliance), Asselineau (two irrelevant sovereigntists), Poutou (the also-ran club)
dupont_aignan.target_audience: Political joke enthusiasts, sovereignty memers, anyone who follows minor candidates for fun

poutou.name: Philippe Poutou
poutou.bio: The NPA (New Anti-Capitalist Party) candidate who ran in 2012 (1.2%), 2017 (0.6%), and 2022 (0.6%). A factory worker turned Trotskyist candidate known for his last-minute presidential bids and anti-capitalist rhetoric. Keeps the revolutionary left flag flying in France despite the NPA's marginal position. The working-class face of anti-establishment politics who actually looks and sounds like what he claims to represent - though his vote totals don't translate to political power.
poutou.meme_summary: The factory worker who shows up to presidential debates like he just clocked off a shift—the most authentically punk politician in France.
poutou.best_takedown_angle: Mock his complete lack of electability while acknowledging his entertainment value. He's there to roast everyone else, not to actually win—political performance art.
visual_gags: The 'I don't belong here and I know it' grin
catchphrases: On n'est pas des professionnels de la politique
Nos vies valent plus que leurs profits
On est là pour foutre le bordel
poutou.combo_partners: Macron (worker vs banker, the ultimate contrast), Le Pen (called out her privilege on live TV), Arthaud (the far-left debate tag team)
poutou.target_audience: Anti-establishment memers, debate highlight reel fans, anyone who enjoys watching politicians get roasted

arthaud.name: Nathalie Arthaud
arthaud.bio: The Lutte Ouvrière candidate and organizational leader who runs for president like clockwork every five years (2012: 0.4%, 2017: 0.6%, 2022: 0.6%). The Trotskyist party's presidential placeholder who maintains the organization's presence in mainstream politics through her bids. Unlike Poutou's NPA, LO remains strictly Leninist in structure and refuses any electoral alliances. The most 'on-brand' revolutionary: always running, never winning, maintaining the fiction that the working class will rise up any election now.
arthaud.meme_summary: The Trotskyist teacher who grades capitalism an F every election cycle—so consistent she could be a political screensaver.
arthaud.best_takedown_angle: Mock her robotic consistency—she says the exact same things every election like a political Groundhog Day. The revolution is always 'coming soon' but never arrives.
visual_gags: The exact same speech, different year
Revolutionary poster that hasn't changed since 1970
Teaching class about the means of production
catchphrases: Le capitalisme est un système à abattre
Les travailleurs n'ont pas de patrie
arthaud.combo_partners: Poutou (the far-left debate duo), Mélenchon (mainstream left vs revolutionary left), Macron (capitalism incarnate vs its nemesis)
arthaud.target_audience: Political meme enthusiasts, debate fans, anyone amused by revolutionary consistency

asselineau.name: François Asselineau
asselineau.bio: The UPR (Union Populaire Républicaine) leader who runs for president every cycle (2017: 0.9%, 2022: 0.5%). A hardline Frexit and anti-EU candidate who wants France to leave the EU and NATO. His platform is essentially France-first nationalism combined with euroskepticism that's slightly to the right of Zemmour but with less media presence. The persistent fringe candidate who keeps the 'Frexit' dream alive despite zero institutional support.
asselineau.meme_summary: The Frexit guy who can connect any topic to leaving the EU in under 30 seconds—ask him about the weather and he'll blame Brussels.
asselineau.best_takedown_angle: Mock his monomaniacal focus on one issue and his conspiracy-adjacent worldview. He's the political version of that relative who turns every family dinner into a rant about the same topic.
visual_gags: Connecting random events to the EU with red string
EU flag with a giant X through it
catchphrases: Sortir de l'UE, de l'euro et de l'OTAN
L'UPR est le parti dont les médias ne parlent pas
Tout s'explique par les traités européens
Vous ne savez pas ce qu'on vous cache
asselineau.combo_partners: Dupont-Aignan (the sovereigntist loser club), Le Pen (Frexit vs soft Euroscepticism), Mélenchon (two conspiracy-adjacent worldviews)
asselineau.target_audience: Conspiracy enthusiasts, EU skeptics, YouTube political rabbit hole dwellers

larcher.name: Gérard Larcher
larcher.bio: The Senate President since 2014 who just keeps winning re-election to the upper chamber seat from Yvelines. The Gaullist elder statesman who presides over the Senate with traditional authority while France's political system burns below. At 76, he's seen as a stabilizing figure representing the 'third way' between extremes. Has survived multiple presidents and prime ministers, remaining the constant in French political chess. The wise elder whose main job now is maintaining senatorial traditions while everyone else loses their minds.
larcher.meme_summary: The Senate grandpa who's been in power so long he might have been there when they built the Palais du Luxembourg—France's most powerful man nobody under 40 has heard of.
larcher.best_takedown_angle: Mock his age, irrelevance to young people, and the Senate itself—a chamber that most French people couldn't explain if you paid them. He's the president of a building most people think is a park.
visual_gags: The Senate that looks like a retirement home
Institutional furniture that's younger than him
catchphrases: Le Sénat est le gardien des libertés
larcher.combo_partners: Braun-Pivet (the two chamber presidents nobody cares about), Macron (institutional resistance vs presidential ambition), Wauquiez (LR old guard vs new guard)
larcher.target_audience: Political insiders, institutional humor fans, anyone who thinks the Senate is useless

knafo.name: Sarah Knafo
knafo.role: Eurodéputée Reconquête, Candidate à la mairie de Paris 2026
knafo.bio: Born in 1993, this former magistrate at the Cour des Comptes (2020-2024) became Éric Zemmour's chief strategist and romantic partner during his 2022 presidential campaign. Elected MEP in 2024 on the Reconquête list led by Marion Maréchal, she was the number 3 candidate. In January 2026, she announced her candidacy for Paris mayor, positioning the capital as a springboard toward 2027. At 32, she embodies the new generation of the radical right—overqualified, over-ambitious, and over-exposed. Politico named her one of '10 to watch in 2026.' She's TikTok-active in the 2026 municipal campaign and rivals Dati for Paris. The irony: a top-of-the-class magistrate running as an 'anti-system' candidate for a populist party.
knafo.meme_summary: The overachieving magistrate who became Zemmour's girlfriend AND political heir—like graduating top of your class and then joining the rebellion.
knafo.archetype: The elite populist / Power couple strategist
knafo.best_takedown_angle: Mock the absurdity of a Cour des Comptes magistrate running as an anti-establishment candidate. She's the ultimate elite pretending to be anti-elite—like a Rolex wearing a Casio costume.
visual_gags: Cour des Comptes robes next to campaign posters
The overachiever CV vs populist rhetoric
Standing next to Zemmour looking like the real candidate
catchphrases: Paris décline, l'urgence est à l'action
Je suis magistrate, je sais compter
La droite a besoin de sang neuf
knafo.combo_partners: Zemmour (partner and mentor—the ultimate power couple), Dati (rival for Paris mayor, both under legal clouds), Marion Maréchal (the Reconquête women's civil war), Chikirou (LFI's Paris candidate—left vs far-right for the capital)
knafo.target_audience: Far-right youth, Reconquête supporters, anti-Macron conservatives, political gossip fans

praud.name: Pascal Praud
praud.role: Journaliste, Animateur de L'Heure des Pros sur CNews
praud.bio: Former sports journalist who became France's most influential—and divisive—political TV host. Since 2017, he's hosted 'L'Heure des Pros' on CNews, turning it into France's most-watched political talk show and making CNews the #1 news channel in France by 2024, overtaking BFM TV. Acknowledged on-air that CNews has 'proximity to the ideas of Éric Zemmour.' A parody sketch by comedian Malik Bentalha was viewed 33 MILLION times in 2023, making Praud a meme icon. He reportedly turned down a €1 million offer from rival BFM TV. Described by L'Express as 'the most influential journalist in France' in 2025. His show features increasingly radical far-right commentators, drawing regular warnings from media regulator Arcom. Le Monde described him as 'Bolloré's flagship' in a January 2026 investigation.
praud.meme_summary: The sports commentator who became France's Tucker Carlson—turning political debates into entertainment and accidentally becoming a meme through a 33-million-view parody sketch.
praud.archetype: The talk show king / Right-wing megaphone
praud.best_takedown_angle: Mock his transformation from football commentator to political kingmaker. A sports journalist who now shapes France's political debate—as if a wrestling announcer started moderating presidential debates. The Bentalha parody (33M views) is the ultimate takedown template.
visual_gags: The CNews studio as a wrestling ring
Sports commentary gestures during political debates
Phone calls with politicians between takes
praud.combo_partners: Bolloré (the puppet master and his star puppet), Zemmour (CNews's greatest creation), Mélenchon (the eternal enemy), Bock-Côté (his favorite intellectual guest)
praud.target_audience: French right-wing media consumers, CNews viewers, anti-Macron crowd, meme enthusiasts who love the Bentalha parody

payan.name: Benoît Payan
payan.bio: Mayor of Marseille since December 2020, born in 1978 in the working-class Pont-de-Vivaux neighborhood—the genuine local boy who made it to the top. He inherited the job from Michèle Rubirola (who resigned for health reasons) and became the youngest mayor of France's second city. Awarded the Légion d'Honneur in January 2025 for '22 years of service.' His tenure has been defined by the narcotraffic war that plagues Marseille—a 10-year-old was killed by a stray bullet in 2024 in a case linked to drug trafficking. He supports selling the Stade Vélodrome to Olympique de Marseille. In January 2026, he officially announced his re-election bid, running on a broad left coalition WITHOUT LFI. The Marseille municipal race is one of France's most watched, with RN threatening to take the city.
payan.meme_summary: The mayor of France's most chaotic city—where drug wars, football politics, and urban decay collide. Running a city that's basically a Netflix crime series set in real life.
payan.best_takedown_angle: Mock the impossible job—being mayor of Marseille means presiding over drug wars, crumbling infrastructure, and football drama simultaneously. Every headline from Marseille reads like a crime show synopsis.
catchphrases: Il reste encore de grandes choses à accomplir
Je connais les quartiers, j'en viens
payan.combo_partners: Delogu (LFI rival for Marseille), Retailleau (Interior Minister vs Marseille chaos), Hidalgo (two left-wing mayors of France's biggest cities), Darmanin (law and order vs street reality)
payan.target_audience: Marseillais, crime drama fans, political watchers, anyone following the narcotraffic crisis

doucet.name: Grégory Doucet
doucet.bio: The poster child of France's 2020 'green wave,' elected mayor of Lyon in 2020 on an ambitious ecological platform. He declared a 'climate emergency' in Lyon—the first major French city to do so. But in April 2025, he was placed in garde à vue (police custody) as part of an investigation into alleged misuse of public funds—a probe that began with a police raid on Lyon's City Hall in March 2024. Released the same day, but the investigation continues. In November 2025, he launched his re-election campaign calling it a 'joyful campaign'—an interesting choice of words for someone who just spent a day in police custody. He faces Jean-Michel Aulas (the former Lyon football club president) in what promises to be a fascinating clash between ecology and football capitalism.
doucet.meme_summary: The green mayor who declared climate emergency but ended up in a garde à vue for financial misconduct—the irony of 'Mr. Clean' getting his hands dirty.
doucet.archetype: The green mayor / Climate warrior under investigation
doucet.best_takedown_angle: Mock the delicious irony—he declared a 'climate emergency' and ended up in police emergency. The mayor who wants to save the planet but can't save his own accounting. His 'joyful campaign' after a garde à vue is pure comedy gold.
visual_gags: Climate emergency declaration next to police custody photo
The 'joyful campaign' poster with mugshot energy
Green recycling bins... recycling public funds
Lyon City Hall with police tape
catchphrases: Cette campagne, nous la voulons joyeuse
doucet.combo_partners: Aulas (football capitalism vs green politics), Hidalgo (two green mayors, two controversies), Wauquiez (LR regional rival), Bernard (green metro duo)
doucet.target_audience: Anti-green voters, political irony fans, Lyon political watchers, anyone who loves a good hypocrisy story
memes: Garde à vue du maire vert

bollore.name: Vincent Bolloré
bollore.role: Milliardaire, Patron du groupe Bolloré / Empire médiatique
bollore.bio: The Breton billionaire who bought France's right-wing media ecosystem and reshaped the country's political landscape. Owner of CNews (since 2017, turned it into France's #1 news channel by 2024), Europe 1, Le Journal du Dimanche, and part of Canal+. Under his direction, CNews adopted a Fox News model, platforming far-right voices and turning commentators like Zemmour into presidential candidates. Reporters Without Borders has repeatedly flagged CNews for lack of pluralism. His empire is accused of normalizing far-right ideas across multiple media properties—from TV to radio to print magazines like 'Capital,' 'Voici,' and 'Femme actuelle.' An ultraconservative Catholic, he is arguably the most powerful unelected figure in French politics. As The Guardian put it in December 2025: France fears an 'era of Trumpism' fueled by Bolloré's media.
bollore.meme_summary: The puppet master who doesn't need to run for office because he owns the media that decides who does. France's Rupert Murdoch, but with better wine.
bollore.best_takedown_angle: Mock his outsized influence on democracy—he's never been elected to anything but shapes who gets elected through his media empire. The billionaire who bought France's public debate and turned it into a Fox News franchise.
visual_gags: Puppet strings attached to CNews hosts
Fox News logo morphing into CNews
Bolloré at the center of a media spider web
Politicians lining up to appear on his shows
The invisible hand behind every headline
catchphrases: CNews: première chaîne d'info de France
bollore.combo_partners: Praud (his star host), Zemmour (his political creation), Le Pen (his implicit ally), Macron (his target), Nedjar (his operational chief)
bollore.target_audience: Media critics, anti-oligarch voters, left-wing activists, anyone worried about media concentration

bock_cote.name: Mathieu Bock-Côté
bock_cote.bio: A Québécois sociologist and essayist who became one of France's most prominent right-wing intellectual commentators. A regular fixture on CNews's 'L'Heure des Pros' and columnist for Le Figaro, he specializes in identity politics, immigration criticism, and denouncing 'wokisme.' In November 2024, he was the first to frame a news incident as having a 'dimension of conquest'—illustrating CNews's tendency to give far-right framing to breaking news. The ultimate irony: a Canadian immigrant has become France's loudest voice against immigration. His intellectual veneer provides academic cover for positions that would sound crude from anyone without a PhD.
bock_cote.meme_summary: The Canadian immigrant who became France's top anti-immigration intellectual—because nothing says 'close the borders' like someone who crossed an ocean to be here.
